name: Wince
classification_notes: Wince is built on a sharp bitmap grid with pixelated letterforms, placing it firmly in the display category as a pixel or bitmap typeface. Its varying widths and playful contrast give it an unorthodox, decorative character beyond functional text use.
summary: A bold bitmap display typeface that pairs a pixelated grid structure with playful width variation and unorthodox letterforms.
description: Built on a sharp bitmap structure, Wince softens its retro pixel aesthetic with varying character widths and an irreverent approach to letterform design. It sits in the display space where digital nostalgia meets deliberate eccentricity, suited to titles, posters and screen-native contexts. Available as a single OTF file.
foundry_blurb: Double Dagger describe Wince as a bold typeface that fuses a classic pixelated aesthetic with modern quirks, noting its sharp bitmap structure, playful contrast, varying widths and unorthodox design as what sets it apart in digital design.

name: Nome
classification_notes: A heavyweight, uniform display sans with blocky letterforms and angled joints; positioned explicitly as a varsity-style alternative, which places it firmly in the display category rather than a functional sans-serif.
summary: A blocky heavyweight display sans with angled joints, positioned as a modern alternative to varsity lettering styles.
description: Built around uniform, weighty letterforms with precisely chiseled details and tightly angled joints that give it more crispness than a typical varsity face. Available as a single OTF file, it targets headline and identity work where strong presence is needed without the nostalgic roughness of traditional collegiate typefaces. A compact, single-weight release from Double Dagger suited to posters, logotypes and short-run display settings.
foundry_blurb: Double Dagger describe Nome as a heavyweight sans with a strong presence, noting its uniform, blocky design as clean and classic while its precise chiseling and tightly angled joints create a striking impact. They position it as a modern, eye-catching alternative to traditional varsity fonts.

name: Gelati
classification_notes: The foundry describes it as a semi-serif combining soft corners with angular strokes; the bold, decorative letterforms and display intent place it firmly in the display category rather than a conventional serif or sans-serif classification.
summary: A semi-serif display typeface mixing soft rounded corners with angular strokes across bold, contemporary letterforms.
description: Gelati sits between serif and sans-serif, applying selective serifs to otherwise angular and bold letterforms. The contrast between soft corners and sharp strokes gives individual glyphs a distinct character suited to headline and display use. Available as a free OTF download direct from the foundry.
foundry_blurb: Double Dagger describe Gelati as a semi-serif typeface that blends soft corners with angular strokes, noting that subtle variation on otherwise conventional bold letterforms makes it a standout contemporary display font.

name: Monos
classification_notes: Described as a blocky serif with condensed, irregular forms and hand-carved character, placing it firmly in the display serif category with vernacular or woodtype influences.
summary: A blocky, condensed display serif with hand-carved irregularity that leans into woodtype nostalgia without feeling overwrought.
description: Monos is a single-weight display serif with condensed proportions and deliberate irregularity in its forms, giving it the texture of hand-lettering or carved type. The blocky construction and friendly character make it well-suited to branding, poster work or logotype uses where warmth is more useful than precision. Available as a single OTF file at a low price point.
foundry_blurb: Double Dagger describe Monos as a blocky serif with hand-carved charm, noting its condensed, irregular forms as equally nostalgic and friendly.

name: Wraith Mono
classification_notes: An all-caps monospaced font with chunky, soft letterforms; classified as geometric mono due to its uniform spacing and simplified, rounded construction.
summary: An all-caps monospaced font with chunky, rounded letterforms suited to both strong display headings and tabular settings.
description: Simple in construction, Wraith Mono pairs chunky weight with softened forms, giving it a character that works across scales. Its monospaced design makes it serviceable for tabular data while the bold presence holds up at headline sizes. Available as a single OTF file at a low price point.
foundry_blurb: Double Dagger describe Wraith Mono as simple in form and function, chunky yet soft, and suited to both large, strong headings and smaller tabular uses. It is an all-caps monospaced font available as a digital OTF download.

name: Sancta
classification_notes: Sancta is a display typeface drawing on Gaelic calligraphic traditions, with geometric interpretations of uncial letterforms. Its Celtic heritage and decorative intent place it firmly in display rather than text use.
summary: A geometric Celtic display font drawing on Gaelic calligraphic tradition with clean lines and sharp corners.
description: Built around the visual language of Gaelic calligraphy, Sancta trades in the rounded warmth of traditional uncials for a sharper, more geometric approach. The result is a Celtic display face that reads as contemporary rather than antiquarian, suited to headings, packaging and branding where a legible nod to Celtic heritage is wanted. Sold as a single OTF file at a low price point, it is a compact, single-purpose offering.
foundry_blurb: Double Dagger describe Sancta as a Celtic display font featuring clean lines, sharp corners and a distinct geometric style. They say it embodies the essence of Gaelic calligraphy while meeting the demands of contemporary design, with a balance struck between tradition and readability.

name: Poolside
classification_notes: Inspired by popular serifs of the 1980s and 90s used in headlines and advertising, Poolside sits in display serif territory with expressive, period-specific character forms suited to titles rather than body text.
summary: A display serif drawn from the headline and advertising typefaces of the 1980s and 90s, offered in Regular and Italic.
description: Poolside takes its cues from the popular serif styles that dominated titles and advertising during the 1980s and 90s. Certain expressive glyphs distinguish it from straightforward period revivals, giving it a slightly edgier character. Available as a free download in two styles, Regular and Italic, in OTF format.
foundry_blurb: Double Dagger describe Poolside as inspired by popular serifs of the 80s and 90s, used heavily in titles, headlines and advertisements. They note that certain expressive glyphs make it stand out from alternatives, and position it as useful for both sophisticated and edgy designs. It is offered in Regular and Italic styles.

name: Courtside
classification_notes: Tall, condensed letterforms with low contrast and sharp corners place this firmly in display territory, evoking 90s sports and tech aesthetics rather than text-setting traditions.
summary: A tall condensed display face with sharp corners and low contrast, drawing on 1990s sports and tech design aesthetics.
description: Built around tall, condensed letterforms with sharp corners and low contrast, it captures the streamlined efficiency associated with 90s sports branding and forward-thinking graphic design. Available in just two styles, Regular and Italic, the family is lean but purposeful. Works well for headlines, posters and sports-adjacent identities where a retro-professional edge is wanted.
foundry_blurb: Double Dagger describe Courtside as recalling the 90s era of streamlined efficiency and forward-thinking design, citing its tall, condensed letterforms and sleek lines as embodying a sporty, dynamic vibe. Low contrast and sharp corners give it a polished, nostalgic yet professional finish, offered in Regular and Italic styles.

name: Alizarin
classification_notes: Described as a serif with a hand-drawn quality and subtle asymmetry, drawing on print references; the organic irregularity places it in a hand-drawn serif subcategory rather than a conventional text or display serif.
summary: A serif with deliberate hand-drawn asymmetry, referencing the imperfections found in printed matter to give it a contemporary, tactile quality.
description: Alizarin draws on the irregularities of print as a source, building subtle asymmetry into each letterform to avoid the mechanical evenness of conventional digital serifs. The result sits between a refined text face and a display serif, lending warmth and character to editorial or branding contexts where a purely constructed form would feel too cold. Available as a single OTF file and offered free of charge.
foundry_blurb: Double Dagger describe Alizarin as a sophisticated serif with a hand-drawn style, imitating references found in print. Its subtle asymmetry, they say, results in a contemporary appearance and function.

name: DD Sans
classification_notes: Described explicitly as circular and geometric, placing it squarely in the geometric sans tradition alongside faces like Futura or Circular.
summary: A circular geometric sans in three weights, sold as a general-purpose OTF download for $25.
description: Three weights of a clean, circular geometric sans with no stated italic or condensed cuts. Positioned as broadly applicable, though the sparse product copy gives little away about its distinguishing characteristics. At $25 for an OTF download, it sits at the budget end of the independent foundry market.
foundry_blurb: Double Dagger describe DD Sans as a clean, circular geometric sans serif offered in three weights and suitable for a limitless number of applications, available as a digital OTF download after purchase.

name: Drafter
classification_notes: All-caps, bold display typeface inspired by hand-painted signage, placing it firmly in the display category with vernacular lettering roots rather than a formal sans-serif tradition.
summary: A bold, all-caps display face drawn from hand-painted sign lettering, built for high-impact headline use.
description: Inspired by the tradition of hand-painted signage, Drafter is a single-weight, all-caps typeface designed to draw attention at large sizes. Its vernacular roots give it a directness suited to posters, packaging and short-form display work. Supplied as a single OTF file, it is a focused, no-frills tool for bold typographic statements.
foundry_blurb: Double Dagger describe Drafter as a bold, all-caps typeface inspired by hand-painted signage, built to stand out, and available as a digital OTF download.

name: Mazer
classification_notes: Mazer is classified as display due to its hybrid nature, blending geometric sans construction with monospaced serif elements and sharp angular turns that make it unsuitable for body text.
summary: A hybrid display face that crosses geometric sans proportions with monospaced serif construction and sharp angular turns.
description: Mazer sits at an unusual intersection, pairing the even spacing logic of a monospaced serif with the clean geometry of a sans. Sharp corners give it an angular, almost technical character that separates it from softer geometric display faces. Available as a single OTF file, it suits headlines and short display settings where its structural oddity reads as intent.
foundry_blurb: Double Dagger describe Mazer as a typeface characterised by sharp turns that bends conventions by combining elements of a geometric sans and a monospaced serif to create a unique display typeface.

name: Dextur
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a modern interpretation of traditional blackletter scripts with calligraphic and rugged qualities, placing it firmly in the blackletter category.
summary: A calligraphic blackletter with a rugged edge, aimed at classical and grungy aesthetics.
description: Dextur takes traditional blackletter scripts as its starting point and applies a rougher, more contemporary sensibility. The calligraphic construction keeps it legible and rooted in historical letterforms, while the rugged texture makes it suitable for editorial or branding work that sits outside clean, polished territory. Delivered as a single OTF file, it is a straightforward, affordable option for display use.
foundry_blurb: Double Dagger describe Dextur as a modern interpretation of traditional blackletter scripts, noting its calligraphic and rugged character and positioning it for those with classical or grungy artistic tastes.

name: 2023 Bundle
classification_notes: A bundle of five distinct typefaces across varied styles; no single category applies, though the naming conventions and foundry aesthetic suggest display-oriented design work throughout.
summary: A discounted bundle of five 2023 releases from Double Dagger, plus an exclusive italic companion for Alizarin Regular.
description: Collects five full-glyph-set typefaces from Double Dagger's 2023 output: Mazer, Wraith, Monos, Sancta and Dextur. The bundle also includes the italic companion to Alizarin Regular, available only through this purchase. Delivered as OTF files and priced at 40% off the combined individual cost.
foundry_blurb: Double Dagger offer their 2023 Bundle at a 40% saving on the combined retail price, packaging five releases with complete glyph sets alongside the Alizarin Italic, which is exclusive to this bundle and not sold separately.
